ety: S. तन्त्र+कः
def: s.m. A large bamboo or wickerwork frame; a matted shutter; a screen; a lurking or hiding place
subs: (-meṇ), To be enclosed, to be surrounded; to be thronged or crowded (a door or shop).

def: s.m. prop. ṭaṭṭā, q.v.
s.m. = ṭaṭṭā and ṭhaṭṭar, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), Hot, warm; fervent, zealous; passionate, wrathful, furious, outrageous.
s.f. A joke, jest; a lie; — a kettledrum.

ety: see ṭaṭānā
def: v.n. To pain or ache intensely or much (the limbs or body).

ety: S. तन्त्र+इका
def: s.f. A frame-work of bamboo (used for illumination, or as a support for a vine or other creeper, or to form an enclosure, &c.); a matted frame (for a door or window); a screen; a fence; a hiding place; a necessary office, privy, latrine (such being usually enclosed by matted bamboo frames); — ṭaṭṭī bandhnā (-kī), To be drawn up or formed in close rank (men): ṭaṭṭī jānā, v.n. To go to the necessary, to go to the rear, to perform a function of nature
subs: v.n. To sit behind a screen; to do (anything) secretly; to form an ambuscade
(or āṛ-meṅ) shikār khelnā, v.n. To hunt or shoot from behind a screen
(-meṅ), To put up a screen or shutter; to screen; to enclose, to fence
or ḵẖas-kī ṭaṭṭī, s.f. A bamboo frame covered with the fragrant Andropogon grass (it is placed in a door-way and kept constantly wet with the view of cooling the air as it enters the house)
s.f. A screen used by sportsman to conceal themselves in approaching game; a screen or cover behind which anything can be done in secrecy.

ety: Prk. तट्टुओ; S. तर (rt. तॄ) + तृ + कः
def: s.m. An undersized horse, a pony.
s.m. prop. ṭaṭṭū, q.v.

ety: tad+twa
def: s.m. The state of being that, true state, real state, truth, reality, essential nature, essence, elementary property, first principle, primary fact, axiom; substance, sum and substance, pith, gist, cream; the real nature of the human soul (as being one and the same with the supreme spirit pervading the universe); mind, intellect; — slow time in music; a musical instrument
subs: adj. According to the sense or purport of what has been said

vulg. tatwa-gya, adj. & s.m. Knowing the truth, knowing the true nature of anything; acquainted with the true principles of philosophy; understanding the principles of a science thoroughly; — a profoundly learned or skilful person, &c.
vulg. tatwa-gyān, s.m. Knowledge of the truth; a thorough knowledge of the principles of a science; insight into the true principles of philosophy
adj. Connected with true principles, &c. (see tattwa)
s.m. Ink; the stalk of the Nyctanthes tristis.

def: s.f. (local) = ṭaṭwānī, q.v. s.f. A pony mare.

ety: ṭaṭṭū+S. aff. आनी
def: s.f. A pony mare.

ety: inf. n. ii of طول 'to be extended, &c.
def: s.f. Extending, stretching out, lengthening, prolonging; prolongation, prorogation.

ety: S. तात+गुः
def: s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.

ety: acc. of طوع 'being submissive'
def: adv. Willingly
subs: adv. Willingly or unwillingly nolens volens.

def: s.f. Vowing to sin no more; repenting; penitence, repentance; conversion; abjuring, renouncing; recantation; — tauba, or tauba-tauba, intj. Fie!, faugh!, horrible!, good gracious!, never again!, Heaven forfend!
subs: adv. In all penitence; appealing to God, before God (I declare, &c.)
s.f. The sound of cries under punishment; — tauba-dhāṛ karnā, v.n. To shriek or yell under punishment
v.t. To make (one) repent; to entreat or induce (one) to repent
(-se), To vow to sin no more; to repent (of evil, sin, crime, &c.); to recant; to abjure, renounce, forswear.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوْبَه (tawba), from Arabic تَوْبَة (tawba).

def: s.f. corr. of tauba, q.v. s.f. Vowing to sin no more; repenting; penitence, repentance; conversion; abjuring, renouncing; recantation; — tauba, or tauba-tauba, intj. Fie!, faugh!, horrible!, good gracious!, never again!, Heaven forfend!
subs: adv. In all penitence; appealing to God, before God (I declare, &c.)
s.f. The sound of cries under punishment; — tauba-dhāṛ karnā, v.n. To shriek or yell under punishment
v.t. To make (one) repent; to entreat or induce (one) to repent
(-se), To vow to sin no more; to repent (of evil, sin, crime, &c.); to recant; to abjure, renounce, forswear.

ety: inf. n. ii of وبخ not in use
def: s.f. Threatening, speaking harshly to; reproof, rebuke, reproach.

ety: inf. n. ii of ودع 'to bid adieu'
def: s.f. Bidding adieu (to), taking leave (of); dismissing.

ety: inf. n. of طوف 'to go round,' &c.
def: s.m. Going round, making the circuit (of), perambulating; surrounding; — pilgrimage; the procession round the temple of Mecca.

ety: inf. n. ii of وفق 'to find convenient and useful'
def: s.f. Making events to conspire happily (Divine Providence); divine guidance, grace, or favour; the completion of one's wishes; prosperity; — ability, power, means, resources.

ety: inf. n. ii of وفر 'to be full, to abound'
def: s.f. Making complete or perfect; increasing, multiplying; completion; increase, augmentation; abundance; excess, surplus, savings; — perquisites, pickings; — adj. In excess; supernumerary.
adv. See s.v. tau. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.

ety: inf. n. ii of وحد 'to be single or alone'
def: s.f. Declaring (God) to be one alone; believing in the unity of God; unity; unitarianism.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَوْحِيد (tawḥīd).

ety: inf. n. ii of وهن 'to be weak'
def: s.f. Debilitating, enervating, relaxing; scoffing.
ex: اِس گانے کی توہین کیوں کرنا چاہتے ہو؟
is gāne ki tauhīn kyū̃ karnā cāhte ho?
Why do you want to massacre the song?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian توهین (tawhīn), from Arabic تَوْهِين (tawhīn), verbal noun of وَهَّنَ (wahhana, “to debilitate, to dishearten”).

def: adv. See s.v. tau. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.

ety: inf. n. ii of وجه
def: s.f. Calling one's attention (to); explaining, accounting for; illustrating; illustration; argument; adjustment of accounts; assessment; a statement, a description-roll

ety: v.n. fr. طوق 'to be able to do, or to bear,' &c.
def: s.m. A neck-ring; a collar (of gold, &c., for ornament; or of iron, &c. for punishment; or worn as a badge of servitude); a necklace; a yoke
subs: or t̤auq-ě-zanjīr, s.m. Irons and fetters, manacles
v.t. To put in irons, to imprison.

ety: inf. n. ii of وقع 'to fall'
def: s.f. Signing (an order, &c.) with the royal signet; the royal signet put to diplomas, letters patent, and other public deeds.

ety: inf. n. ii of وکل
def: s.f. Keeping in custody, imprisonment.

ety: inf. n. ii of وقر 'to be sober or grave,' &c.
def: s.f. Honouring, revering, respecting, treating with ceremony; honour, reverence, veneration, respect
s.m. = taulī, q.v. s.f. A vessel used by Hindūs (orig. applied to a copper vessel, syn. with tambiyā; but now also applied to an earthen vessel).
s.m. An intoxicating liquor made from the flowers of the Mahuʼā tree.
s.f. = tulwāʼī, q.v. s.f. The act o weighing; price paid for having (a thing) weighed, weighment fee.

ety: caus. of tolnā
def: v.t. = tulwānā, q.v. v.t. To have (a thing) weighed, &c. = tulānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be weighed, to have or get (a thing) weighed, valued, &c. ( = taulānā, tulwānā).

ety: S. ताम्रिका
def: s.f. A vessel used by Hindūs (orig. applied to a copper vessel, syn. with tambiyā; but now also applied to an earthen vessel).

ety: inf. n. ii of ولد 'to bear children'
def: s.f. Procreating, begetting; assisting at a birth, doing the work of a midwife; generation, birth; production; growth.

ety: corr. from the English
def: s.m. A towel.

ety: S. ताम्रिका
def: s.f. A vessel used by Hindūs (orig. applied to a copper vessel, syn. with tambiyā; but now also applied to an earthen vessel).

ety: for A. تولية, inf. n. ii of ولي 'to assist; to govern'
def: s.f. Appointing (one) as governor, deputy, or superintendent; setting over; the superintendence (of any business); trusteeship
subs: s.m. Letter or order appointing one a superintendent, &c.; deed of trusteeship.

def: s.f. = tulwāʼī, q.v. s.f. The act o weighing; price paid for having (a thing) weighed, weighment fee.

ety: Ap. Prk. तेवडु, Prk. तेद्दहो; S. तावत्
def: pron. correl. (of jaun), He, she, it, that, that same ( = so).

ety: v.n. fr. طعن; see t̤āʻin, and t̤aʻn
def: s.m. An epidemic, a plague, pestilence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طاعُون (tā'ūn), from Arabic طَاعُون (ṭāʕūn).

ety: S. तपुस्
def: s.f. Heat of the sun; heat; great thirst.
s.f. = tauṅs, q.v. s.f. Heat of the sun; heat; great thirst.
s.m. Sun-stroke.
v.n. To be heated in the sun: to be overcome by heat, to be faint or languid with heat; to be parched; to droop, fade, wither; to be very thirsty.

ety: v.n. fr. طور 'to hover round'
def: s.m. State, condition, quality; kind, sort; manner, mode, way; conduct, demeanour
subs: v.n. To be in a bad way, or in a wretched condition; to be at the point of death; to be badly treated
s.m. Way of acting, manner, deportment; practice, habit, manner of dealing
adv. In the way, or by way (of); as (e.g. ba-t̤auṛ-ě-māmūl, as usual, regularly, &c.)
In his (or her, or their, &c.) own way; of itself, of himself, of themselves; personally.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic طُور (ṭūr).

def: s.f. = tauret, q.v. s.f. The Pentateuch; the Old Testament.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوْرَات (tawrāt), from Arabic تَوْرَاة (tawrāh).

ety: for A. tauṛāt
def: s.f. The Pentateuch; the Old Testament.

ety: prop. طاؤوس, fr. طوس 'to be beautiful'; cf. Ch. טום; and Gr. ταὼς
def: s.m. A peacock.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَاوُوس (tāwūs), borrowed from Arabic طَاوُوس (ṭāwūs).

def: s.m. A young unbroken horse; a high-blooded, noble steed, war-horse, charger, steed, horse; a plump horse; a hard-mouthed or unmanageable horse.

ety: inf. n. ii of وشح; but derived from wishāḥ 'a belt or girdle'
def: s.f. Putting a belt or girdle (on); adorning; arranging verses so that the initials of the lines being put together may form some word or verse; an acrostic.

ety: fr. t̤āʼūs
def: adj. Of or relating to the peacock; adorned with peacock's feathers, or with jewels, &c. to resemble a peacock's tail (as a throne); — s.f. A kind of ornamental handwriting.

ety: inf. n. ii of وصف 'to describe,' &c.
def: s.f. Describing; qualifying, description; commendation; a qualified substantive (in Gram.).

ety: inf. n. ii of وزع 'to check; to bring together'
def: s.f. A paper in which the different separate payments made by a zamindār, &c. are entered; statement, account; rent-roll; descriptive roll.

ety: inf. n. ii of وضع 'to place or lay down'
def: s.f. Putting down; depressing; humbling, lowering.

ety: inf. n. ii of وضح 'to be clear, evident,' &c.
def: s.f. Making clear or evident; manifestation; illustrating; publishing; statement, account of collections ( = tauzīʻ).
s.m. = tawā, q.v.
adj. Thy, thine ( = terā: com. in H. poetry).
ex: اے خدا تو ہی میرا مددگار ہے!
ai xudā tū-hī merā madadgār hai!
O God, only Thou art my helper!
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुहुं (tuhũ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑀁 (tumaṃ), 𑀢𑀼𑀁 (tuṃ), from Sanskrit त्वम् (tvam).

ety: S. तापकः; cf. tāwā, & the P. tāba
def: s.m. An iron plate or pan on which break-cakes are baked, a griddle; a frying-pan; the small plate or shard in a ćilam on which the tobacco is placed; a shard placed over the tobacco in the ćilam; a leaden pan let down into a well to cool the water, or to check excessive flow; — bottom, or bed (of a river, &c.); layer, stratum; an iron mirror
subs: s.f. lit. 'A drop of water from off a baking-pan'; anything trifling or insignificant, a mere trifle; a transient or fleeting thing
s.m. The falling of sparks from a tawā (regarded as a good omen)
adj. lit. 'An inverted tawā'; coal-black, jet-black.
wety: From Sanskrit तापक (tāpaka). Cognate with Persian تابه (tâbe) and تاوه (tâve).

def: s.m. pl. (of tāběʻ, q.v.), Followers, dependants; &c.

ety: inf. n. of طوف 'to go or walk round,' &c.
def: s.f. Going round, turning; circumambulating; encompassing; making the circuit (of a holy place, as Mecca, &c.); pilgrimage
subs: (-kī), To go round; to make the circuit (of a holy place).

ety: inf. n. vi of وفق 'to find (a thing) convenient,' &c.
def: s.m. Concord, harmony, agreement, concurrence, coincidence; (in Arith.) the relation between two numbers which have a common measure, commensurability.

ety: inf. n. v. of وهم 'to think, imagine,' &c.
def: s.m. Thinking, imagining, supposing; imagination, fancy, supposition, conjecture; suspicion; imputation.

ety: inf. n. v of وحش 'to fly in terror'
def: s.m. Being desolate, being deserted (a city, &c.); feeling desolate or scared; flying (from), aversion, horror.
